items.title: Crisis brings opportunity: Malaysia must step up as a credible safe haven for global funds, says Tengku Zafrul
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 24 — Malaysia must position itself as a credible and compelling alternative as global investors reassess risk amid the crisis in West Asia, said Malaysian Investment Development Authority (Mida) chairman Tengku Datuk Seri Zafrul Abdul Aziz. However, Tengku Zafrul said history shows that periods of disruption reorder the global economy, with capital shifting, supply chains realigning and investors reassessing risk — creating strategic openings that Malaysia must be ready to seize. “If investors are reassessing risk, Malaysia must present itself as a credible and compelling alternative.

items.title: Alvin Ch'ng turns substantial shareholder in Alam Maritim after Maybank exit
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Alam Maritim Resources Bhd global subsea business head Alvin Ch'ng Yi Ming has emerged as a substantial shareholder following the recent exit of Malayan Banking Bhd (Maybank). In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, Ch'ng acquired 39.70 million shares on March 19, representing an 8.91 per cent stake in the group. Meanwhile, global subsea business chief executive officer Datuk Aloysius Albert Michael increased his stake to 55.41 million shares, equivalent to a 12.44 per cent interest, after acquiring additional shares on March 19.

items.title: Denmark votes in close election, outgoing PM tipped to win
items.summary: COPENHAGEN: Danes began voting on Tuesday in general elections, with Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen seen as the favourite after standing up to US President Donald Trump over Greenland. "The alternatives (to Mette Frederiksen) are worse," 24-year-old student Freja Strandlod told AFP just after casting her vote in central Copenhagen. "People may not really like her, but they see her as the right leader," Elisabet Svane, political analyst at Danish newspaper Politiken, told AFP.

items.title: Fire, accident, breakdown slow traffic on major highways
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Traffic on several major highways slowed this afternoon following a vehicle fire, an accident and a lorry breakdown, according to updates from the Malaysian Highway Authority (MHA). The bus bay slip lane was blocked, but traffic remained under control. The MHA, via its official WhatsApp channel, also reported an accident at 3.38pm on the North-South Expressway (E2) at Km98.6 southbound from Yong Peng Utara to Yong Peng Selatan.

items.title: Malaysia calls for ceasefire as Anwar and Luxon swap views on West Asia turmoil and its ripple effects on global security
items.summary: In a Facebook post, Anwar said the stance was conveyed during a telephone conversation with New Zealand Prime Minister Christopher Luxon, with Malaysia continuing to call for an immediate ceasefire, protection of civilians and a resolution through diplomatic channels. “During a telephone conversation with my counterpart from New Zealand, Prime Minister Christopher Luxon, we exchanged views on developments in West Asia and their impact on global security and the economy,” he said. The Prime Minister also welcomed proactive measures taken by the New Zealand government to strengthen energy security and accelerate the transition to renewable energy.

items.title: Ultra-low-cost airlines made risky bet on fuel-efficient planes
items.summary: US carriers on Tuesday said that they were seeing stronger-than-expected spring travel demand. Still, as the US-Israeli war on Iran has pushed US jet fuel prices above US$200 per barrel, legacy carriers are starting to trim capacity like United Airlines or could park aircraft to save on costs, a luxury that Frontier Airlines may not have. Greater fuel efficiency is a clear advantage as airlines' largest expense after labour is jet fuel.

items.title: Police hunt motorcyclist for dangerous firecracker stunt
items.summary: SEGAMAT: Segamat police have launched a manhunt for a motorcyclist whose dangerous antics of lighting and throwing firecrackers while riding sparked outrage after a video of the incident went viral on social media. Segamat deputy police chief, Deputy Superintendent M. Nantha, confirmed that the incident was believed to have taken place at Km185 of Jalan Johor Baru–Seremban at 10.30pm last Sunday. "Police viewed this blatant disregard for road safety and public order with extreme gravity.

items.title: DPM Fadillah clarifies Malaysia not facing ‘water bankruptcy’, but must manage rising demand by 2030
items.summary: KUCHING, March 24 — Malaysia is not at risk of facing “water bankruptcy” as the country still has sufficient raw water resources, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Amar Fadillah Yusof. However, he stressed that the real challenge lies in the integrated management of water resources, protection of river basins, and improving the efficiency of the national water delivery system. Fadillah highlighted several key issues requiring attention, including water storage management, encroachment into catchment areas, river pollution, and the lack of integration among alternative water sources such as groundwater, recycled water, rainwater harvesting and desalination.

items.title: No price tags, misleading pricing, unverified scales: Selangor cracks down on rogue traders at festive bazaars in anti-profiteering sweep
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, March 24 — As Malaysians increasingly spend prudently in response to rising living costs, the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry (KPDN) in Selangor has stepped up enforcement to protect consumers from unfair trading practices. Selangor KPDN director Muhamad Hanif Asa’ari said the ministry’s “Ops Pantau 2026” inspections ran from February 19 and covered 1,284 premises across the state. “Types of offences included failure to display price tags, using unverified weighing instruments and misleading price marking.

items.title: Tel Aviv building damaged in 'direct' hit after Iran missile warning
items.summary: TEL AVIV: The mayor of Tel Aviv said a "direct strike" damaged a building on Tuesday after an Iranian missile attack warning, as Israeli first responders said they had treated four people for light injuries. Tel Aviv mayor Ron Huldai told reporters that the building had been hit by a "direct strike." MDA said it had treated and quickly discharged four people, revising down an earlier toll of six people lightly wounded.

items.title: #NSTViral: Police chase ends in crash through Raya tent in Kulim [WATCH]
items.summary: KULIM: A police pursuit took a dramatic turn when a fleeing Perodua Kancil crashed through a tent outside a house in Padang Serai on the third day of Hari Raya Aidilfitri. Kulim deputy police chief Tengku Mohd Faisal Tengku Yeng confirmed the incident, adding that no injuries were reported. Police are continuing investigations.

items.title: LLM: 2.8 million vehicles expected on highways today
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM) expects some 2.8 million vehicles to flood major highways today as commuters returned to the city centre following the Aidilfitri holidays. Traffic Management Centre supervisor Muhammad Aliff Syafiq Md Takiyudin said the figure represented the highest traffic volume recorded since the start of the festive season. On the Kuala Lumpur-Karak Expressway, traffic volume increased significantly from Bentong heading towards Bukit Tinggi as east coast travellers returned to the Klang Valley.

items.title: No need for panic buying: Hajiji, Armizan address Sabah fuel concerns amid recent rumours
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 24 — Sabah’s petrol, diesel and LPG supply is stable, and the public is urged not to panic buy, federal Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said today. Armizan debriefed Sabah Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor earlier on the outcomes of the Special Committee on Coordination to Combat Leakage and Smuggling Among Enforcement Agencies, which has been activated to strengthen enforcement efforts against fuel smuggling and misappropriation, particularly in border areas. Armizan added that he also addressed several rumours regarding alleged fuel shortages at some petrol stations in Sabah recently.

items.title: Traffic crawls on major expressways after bushfire, vehicle breakdowns
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Motorists across several major highways faced delays today after a string of incidents, including a bushfire, vehicle breakdowns, and a road accident, disrupted traffic flow. Another lorry breakdown at 1.10pm at KM208 of the E2 highway, on the northbound route from Ayer Keroh to Simpang Ampat, also led to slow-moving traffic after the left lane was blocked. Traffic remained under control.

items.title: EPF, SP Setia deny Battersea allegations by former CEO
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Employees Provident Fund (EPF) and SP Setia Bhd have firmly denied allegations made by a former chief executive officer of London's Battersea Power Station, stating they are unfounded and are being robustly contested. In separate statements, both parties clarified that there are no claims by Don O'Sullivan relating to the accounts of Battersea Power Station Development Company (BPSDC). BPSDC manages the Battersea Power Station estate ultimately on behalf of Battersea Project Holding Company, the holding company of the 42-acre regeneration project O'Sullivan, who joined BPSDC in June 2024, claims he was dismissed in May 2025 after raising internal concerns about certain matters at the company.

items.title: Explainer: How the Middle East conflict and China fertiliser curbs are rattling Malaysia’s vegetable supply
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 24 — Malaysia’s vegetable industry, a critical hub for domestic and regional food security, may be facing what food security experts have described as the “perfect storm” of supply chain disruptions. The ‘60 per cent’ vulnerability Malaysia is quite dependent on the global market for its farming nutrients, importing over 60 per cent of its mineral fertiliser requirements, food security expert Fatimah Mohamed Arshad explained. The channel handles nearly 30 per cent of world oil exports, a fifth of the natural gas supply and one-third of global fertiliser exports.

items.title: #NSTviral: Sulking cat over ketupat and rendang steals hearts online [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A cat's adorable "sulking" behaviour after being denied festive food has amused netizens, with a TikTok video garnering more than 1.1 million views. In the clip, the owner said that the cat, named Simba, was upset after not being given ketupat and rendang ayam during Hari Raya Aidilfitri. Another TikTok user wrote: "My cat also likes ketupat and rendang."

items.title: Raya traffic woes: When a five-hour trip turns into 14
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: What should have been a five-hour journey from Kedah to Kuala Lumpur turned into a 14-hour crawl, as one motorist recounted the toll of festive traffic and reminded of the need for better preparation for long drives. Siti Mariam said her trip from Pokok Sena, Kedah, to Setapak quickly descended into gridlock after she exited the Alor Setar Utara toll plaza and joined the North-South Expressway. Despite driving an automatic car, she said the constant stop-and-go traffic left her with aching knees by the time she arrived.

items.title: Penang water supply stable for at least two months as dams, Sungai Muda above alert level despite heatwave
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, March 24 — The Penang Water Supply Corporation (PBAPP) today gave an assurance that the state’s water supply will hold steady for at least two months even amid the ongoing heatwave. All three major dams in Penang are also holding healthy levels, with Air Itam Dam at 73.4 per cent, Teluk Bahang Dam at 86 per cent, and Mengkuang Dam at 91.4 per cent. Pathmanathan said controlled drawdown strategies ensure that the state can sustain water supply for months, with Air Itam Dam lasting up to 72 days (about 2.4 months), Mengkuang 132 days (4.4 months), and Teluk Bahang 261 days (8.7 months) under current usage rates.

items.title: Anwar discusses developments in Middle East conflict with NZ PM
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: In a call with New Zealand Prime Minister Christopher Luxon, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim stressed Malaysia's strong condemnation of the military attack on Iran, emphasising the country's firm rejection of any unilateral actions that violate international law. He said he and Luxon also discussed the impact of the conflict on New Zealand's energy sector, including rising fuel prices and pressure on the domestic economy. "I welcomed the proactive steps taken by the New Zealand government to strengthen energy security and accelerate the transition to renewable energy."

items.title: Philippines warns oil price hike could send inflation soaring, slow growth
items.summary: Before the conflict in the Middle East, the Philippines had expected inflation to accelerate to 3.6 per cent this year from 1.7 per cent in 2025, still within its two to four per cent target, and for the economy to expand five to six per cent from 4.4 per cent last year. Under the most severe scenario, one where oil prices reach US$200 per barrel and stay at that level for six months, inflation could surge sharply, reaching 7.3 per cent to 8.6 per cent for the full year, and hitting as high as 14.3 per cent at one point. Under that scenario, the likelihood of which Balisacan said was "quite low", economic growth could be reduced by 1.47 to 1.95 percentage points to 3.5 per cent to 4.0 per cent.

items.title: A jetty for visitors but not villagers? DBKK urged to open new facility to everyday commuters
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 24 — Kota Kinabalu City Hall (DBKK) must open its newly launched tourism jetty for public use, following growing concerns over the safety of existing facilities relied upon daily by Pulau Gaya residents. Tanjung Aru assemblyman Datuk Junz Wong said the situation faced by islanders commuting to and from the city reflected a harsh reality, with many forced to use ageing, wet, and unsafe jetties. Wong noted that the new DBKK Tourist Jetty under DBKK Holdings is equipped with modern and more comfortable facilities but designated solely for tourism purposes.

items.title: Israeli minister calls for annexation of southern Lebanon
items.summary: BEIRUT: Israel should extend its border with Lebanon up to the Litani River deep inside the country's south, Israel's finance minister said on Monday as Israeli troops bombed bridges and destroyed homes in an escalating military assault. The comments by Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich were the most explicit yet by a senior Israeli official on seizing Lebanese territory in a fight Israel says targets Iran-backed Hizbollah fighters. Lebanon was pulled into the regional war on March 2 when Hizbollah fired missiles into Israel.

items.title: Fire extinguishers, safety courses: Johor plans tougher rules for fireworks traders after Kampung Dato' Sulaiman Menteri blaze
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, March 24 — The Johor government is tightening the rules on the sale of fireworks after a fire broke out at a stall selling them in Kampung Dato’ Sulaiman Menteri here last Sunday that damaged two vehicles nearby. State Housing and Local Government Committee chairman Datuk Mohd Jafni Md Shukor said the proposals include making it mandatory for traders to have safety equipment such as fire extinguishers and to maintain a safe distance from public gathering areas. “The state government sees the need to tighten its existing conditions, including setting sales locations further away from public areas and reviewing the amount and type of firecrackers and fireworks sold.

items.title: Woman, 2 kids die in accident near Dungun, 2 other kids survive
items.summary: DUNGUN: A woman and her two children were killed in an accident near Felda Jerangau here yesterday after attending a wedding. Nurul Hafizah Mohd Shaary, 39, and her son, Muhammad Redzuan Arash Mohamad Redzuan, 13, died at the scene of the incident at Km59 of Jalan Jerangau-Jabor. Nurul Hafizah's two other children, Nur Rakhis Aral, 9, and Nur Raesha Amanda, 14, were injured and taken to Dungun Hospital.

items.title: UN Security Council not likely to approve use of force in Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: PARIS: Bahrain has put forward a draft United Nations Security Council resolution that would authorise countries to use "all necessary means" - diplomatic language for force - to protect commercial shipping in and around the Strait of Hormuz, according to a text seen by Reuters on Monday. Diplomats said the draft text was backed by other Gulf Arab states and the United States, although they said it was unlikely to succeed. The draft resolution calls Iran's actions a threat to international peace and security.

items.title: Oil swings back above US$100 amid conflicting US-Iran signals
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Brent crude has rebounded above US$100 a barrel, a day after plunging more than 10 per cent, amid conflicting signals over potential talks between the United States and Iran. At press time, Brent crude rose 2.67 per cent to US$102.60 a barrel. However, Iran's parliamentary speaker later said no talks had taken place with the US, contradicting Trump's claim.

items.title: New species of marine copepod found in Sipadan's Turtle Tomb
items.summary: SEMPORNA: A new species of marine copepod has been discovered in waters off Pulau Sipadan, adding to understanding of the area's rich marine biodiversity. The 'Peltidium penyu' was identified by a research team led by Associate Professor Dr Azman Abdul Rahim of Universiti Kebangsaan Malaysia during a marine biodiversity survey. The survey was part of the global Ocean Census initiative aimed at accelerating the discovery and documentation of marine life.

items.title: Padang Serai shooting: Suspect with 13 prior convictions held within 24 hours
items.summary: KULIM: A 42-year-old lorry driver arrested over a shooting in Padang Serai has 13 previous convictions linked to crime and drugs, police said. Kulim deputy police chief, Deputy Superintendent Tengku Mohd Faisal Tengku Yeng, said the suspect was detained less than 24 hours after the incident, about 12.30am, in a house in Kampung Naga Lilit. "Checks found the suspect, a local man, has 13 prior convictions related to crime and drugs.

items.title: DBKL to pay parents RM824,180 after fallen tree kills motorcyclist-son, court finds negligence
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 24 — The Sessions Court here has ordered the Kuala Lumpur Mayor to pay RM824,180 in damages to a couple who lost their son after he was killed by a fallen tree on a road divider maintained by Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL). In his grounds of judgment dated March 9, 2026, Effandi Nazila held that the fallen tree was under DBKL’s care and that the incident would not have occurred in the absence of negligence in its monitoring and maintenance. “The tree that fell was under DBKL’s maintenance, and its responsibility is therefore clear and established.

items.title: DBKL ordered to pay RM824,180 over fatal falling tree incident
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Sessions Court here has ordered the Kuala Lumpur Mayor to pay RM824,180 in damages to a couple who lost their son after he was killed by a fallen tree on a road divider maintained by Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL). In his judgment dated March 9, Effandi ruled that the fallen tree was under DBKL's care and that the accident would not have occurred without negligence in monitoring and maintenance. "The tree that fell was under DBKL's maintenance, and its responsibility is therefore clear and established.

items.title: After child falls, Johor to replace guardrails at Desa Mutiara PPR, inspect 15,000 public low-cost flats
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, March 24 — The Johor government is budgeting RM3 million to replace all guardrails at the Desa Mutiara People’s Housing Project (PPR) here, after a boy fell from a second floor unit and suffered heavy injuries. State Housing and Local Government Committee chairman Datuk Mohd Jafni Md Shukor the move involves some 2,000 guardrails and is necessary for the safety of residents. Mohd Jafni said the special allocation is from the state-owned Johor Housing Development Corporation.

items.title: Musk's Starlink denied telecoms licence in Namibia
items.summary: WINDHOEK: Namibia has turned down applications from Elon Musk's satellite internet provider Starlink for a telecommunications service licence and access to radio spectrum, a notice in the Southern African country's government gazette showed. The notice dated March 23 did not say why Starlink's applications were declined. SpaceX, parent company of Starlink, did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

items.title: Repeat offender with 13 convictions nabbed within hours over Kulim shooting, motive still unclear
items.summary: KULIM, March 24 — The suspect in the shooting at Kampung Permatang Durian, Seberang Paya, Padang Serai on March 21 has 13 prior criminal and drug-related convictions. Kulim deputy police chief DSP Tengku Mohd Faisal Tengku Yeng said the 42-year-old lorry driver was arrested within 24 hours of the incident at a house in Kampung Naga Lilit, Padang Serai, at 12.30am. However, he tested negative for drugs,” he said at a press conference at Kulim district police headquarters today.

items.title: NGOs seek update on MACC probe into DoE top officials over e-waste scandal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Two NGOs have urged the government to provide updates on the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission's (MACC) investigation into alleged corruption involving the Department of Environment (DoE) director-general and his deputy. Sahabat Alam Malaysia (SAM) president Meenakshi Raman and Centre to Combat Corruption and Cronyism (C4) chief executive officer Pushpan Murugiah said there have been no updates since MACC submitted investigation papers to the deputy public prosecutor earlier this month with a recommendation for formal charges. "Regular updates must be provided to the public, ensuring transparency and reinforcing confidence in Malaysia's anti-corruption and environmental protection institutions.

items.title: 'Modern beggars' amass easy wealth through social media donation scams
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Thousands of Malaysians are believed to be amassing easy wealth by acting as "modern beggars," soliciting public donations through live-streaming sessions on social media platforms. Exploiting the public's desire to perform charitable acts, these solicitors often use emotional narratives and religious sentiments to drive donations. "Social media allows anyone to plead for money without transparency or accountability.

items.title: Firefighters contain bush fire in Tatau near Bintulu after second day of operations
items.summary: BINTULU, March 24 — The Tatau Fire and Rescue Station (BBP) team prevented a bush fire from spreading further in an area covering about 1.62 hectares along the Kuala Tatau-Serupai coastal stretch in Tatau today. The Sarawak Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) Operations Centre, in a statement, said that the recurring fire was brought under control on the second day of the operation. Seven personnel were involved in the operation, supported by one Light Fire Rescue Tender (LFRT) and four-wheel-drive vehicles.

items.title: Govt rolls out ‘Rakan Tani’ AI farming tool to boost yields, cut rice import gap by 2030
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 24 — Malaysia has begun the nationwide rollout of Rakan Tani, a homegrown artificial intelligence (AI) co-pilot that delivers real-time, personalised farming advice to padi farmers via WhatsApp. The initiative is a collaboration between the Ministry of Digital, Ministry of Agriculture and Food Security, National AI Office (Naio), Padiberas Nasional Berhad (Bernas) and Global AI Village (Gaiv). In a statement, the Ministry of Digital said that Rakan Tani, developed through the NAIO Lab, is a locally built solution designed to tackle agricultural challenges while accelerating AI adoption in line with the AI Nation 2030 vision.

items.title: Fireworks stall blaze: Probe ongoing, multiple angles under scrutiny
items.summary: Penang Fire and Rescue Department director Mohamad Shoki Hamzah said the forensic unit is conducting a detailed probe to determine the cause of the blaze. Shoki said several samples have been collected from the scene and sent for laboratory testing. Shoki said the investigation is expected to be completed within 14 days, although findings may be released earlier if ready.

items.title: Ecological sustainability helps propel the data centre boom
items.summary: LETTER: EVERY time you stream a video, scroll social media, or use AI, your data travels through a data centre. Data centres are rated Tier I to IV based on their reliability and redundancy. Mandating higher-tier facilities ensures new data centres are more sustainable, meeting strict water usage effectiveness targets.

items.title: Police step in as broken-down car adds to post-Raya traffic snarl
items.summary: TAMPARULI: Two traffic police officers helped ease congestion along a busy stretch of Jalan Tamparuli–Ranau after assisting an elderly couple whose vehicle broke down during peak holiday traffic. The Tuaran district police headquarters (IPD) said the officers, Sergeant Larry Gurom Anak Nelson Atang and Sergeant Ridely Tamping, were on duty managing traffic when they spotted the stranded vehicle. Traffic in the area has been heavy since yesterday, particularly along routes linking Kota Kinabalu, Kota Belud, Kota Marudu and Kudat, as well as the Kota Kinabalu–Tamparuli–Ranau road.

items.title: Malaysia as an oil producer: A half-told story, give the government room
items.summary: Regardless of global oil prices, fuel prices in Malaysia (currently subsidised for citizens) are expected to remain cheap. So how do pump prices stay low even when global prices rise? The government 'must' absorb the difference between pump prices and actual market prices by increasing subsidies.

items.title: Anwar backs Indonesia’s bid to cool Iran-Israel conflict, calls for talks and civilian protection
items.summary: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said such support for Jakarta’s initiative is expected to help open the door to meaningful negotiations towards a peaceful and lasting solution in West Asia. In a Facebook post, Anwar said the matter was among the issues discussed during his telephone conversation with Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto yesterday. “We both called for peace, and I informed President Prabowo that Malaysia supports all efforts aimed at achieving peace and resolving the conflict,” he said.

items.title: E-duit raya, QR scams drive 76pc jump in Malaysia’s fraud losses to RM2.7b in 2025
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 24 — Malaysia’s scam-related losses reached approximately RM2.7 billion in 2025, representing a 76 per cent increase from 2024, with a noticeable spike during festive seasons such as Hari Raya, according to cybersecurity firm Fortinet Malaysia. Its country manager, Kevin Wong, said phishing campaigns, fake e-commerce platforms, and fraudulent payment links largely drive these incidents. He said that with the growing adoption of digital payments, including e-duit raya, attackers are also exploiting QR codes and payment channels to redirect transactions.

items.title: Anwar, Prabowo to meet soon, call for peace on Iran–Israel conflict
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto have agreed to meet in the near future to further strengthen bilateral ties and discuss regional stability amid the Iran–Israel conflict. Both leaders also exchanged views on a range of current issues, including the Iran–Israel conflict. Bernama reported that Indonesian Cabinet Secretary Teddy Indra Wijaya said that Prabowo had reached out to several world leaders, including Anwar, Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas, and Egyptian President Abdel Fattah el-Sisi.

items.title: World gave Israel 'licence to torture Palestinians': UN expert
items.summary: GENEVA: The world has given Israel "a licence to torture Palestinians", a UN expert said Monday, with life in the occupied territories "a continuum of physical and mental suffering." Francesca Albanese, the UN's special rapporteur on the rights situation in the Palestinian territories occupied since 1967, alleged that "torture has effectively become state policy" in Israel. "Israel has effectively been given a licence to torture Palestinians, because most of your governments, your ministers, have allowed it," she said, as she presented her latest report to the UN Human Rights Council.

items.title: Three Malaysians among 49 arrested in Phnom Penh raid on global online scam syndicate
items.summary: PHNOM PENH, March 24 — Cambodian authorities have arrested three Malaysians among the 49 suspected members of an online scam syndicate and seized 46 Malaysian SIM cards during a raid in the capital on Sunday. State media Agence Kampuchea Presse reported that the group allegedly used Cambodia as its operational base to target Malaysians and victims in various countries using fake investment schemes. “Authorities said those detained included 43 Chinese nationals, three Malaysians, one Vietnamese woman, one Myanmar woman and one Cambodian woman,” reported the state media this morning.

items.title: Disturbing footage of indecent act in Penang cemetery sparks police probe, two now in custody
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, March 24 — Police have arrested two individuals suspected of engaging in an indecent act at a cemetery in Batu Gantung, Air Itam, after a video of the incident went viral on social media. Timur Laut police chief ACP Abdul Rozak Muhammad said a report was received from a 69-year-old man about the video, which was recorded at a Chinese cemetery. “The first suspect, a 37-year-old woman, was arrested yesterday around 8.35pm at a flat near Jalan Padang Tembak.

items.title: Elderly mum and son found dead in Sg Petani home after more than two days
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI, March 24 — An elderly woman and her son were found dead in their home in Kampung Zainol Abidin, Bedong, yesterday evening. “A report was received at 6.05pm yesterday. Initial inspection found no injuries caused by sharp weapons.

items.title: Johor petrol station under probe after viral videos of subsidised RON95 poured into car boot container
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, March 24 — The Johor Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry (KPDN) is investigating a petrol station in Mutiara Rini here that allegedly allowed two people to fill a container with subsidised RON95 petrol instead of directly into their vehicle tank. Its director Lilis Saslinda Pornomo said investigators will be looking into the petrol station’s closed-circuit television recordings, as well as examining transaction records and purchase receipts for verification purposes. “We found two transactions involving RON95 petrol purchases, with one into the vehicle’s tank, while the other was into a plastic container in the vehicle’s boot with a quantity that exceeded the limit allowed by the Malaysian Supply Controller.

items.title: Your shopping bill is about to get more expensive: Farmers say veggie prices could rise 50pc in April
items.summary: Cameron Highlands Malay Farmers Association chairman Datuk Syed Abd Rahman Syed Abd Rashid said the hike could happen within the next week or two, New Strait Times reported yesterday. Malaysia Muslim Consumers Association chief activist Nadzim Johan advised consumers to spend wisely amid the looming price hikes. “Consumers have the power to influence prices.

items.title: Education Ministry expands School in Hospital programme to Penang, helping students stay on track
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 24 — The Ministry of Education (MOE) is continuously striving to meet the needs of every segment of students, including those undergoing treatment in hospitals, says Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek. Fadhlina said the continuous efforts were also reflected in the Malaysia Education Plan (RPM) 2026-2035, which emphasised inclusive education and equitable access. The SDH programme has been implemented since 2011.

items.title: Pop-up fireworks stall in JB goes up in flames, damages nearby Proton Gen-2 and food truck
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, March 24 — A stall selling firecrackers and fireworks was destroyed in a fire here last Sunday night. Larkin Fire and Rescue Station (BBP) operations commander, Senior Fire Officer I Nakish Nekmat said 11 personnel were rushed to the fire at Majidee Corner 1C, Jalan Bunga Ros, Kampung Dato Sulaiman Menteri, after receiving an emergency call at 8.32 pm. “The pop-up tent used for selling the firecrackers and fireworks was 90 per cent destroyed,” he said in a statement, adding that the fire was brought under control at 8.42 pm, and mop-up operations are currently ongoing.

items.title: Mum and two children dead, two others hurt in Perodua Aruz skid crash near Dungun
items.summary: DUNGUN, March 24 — A mother and her two children were killed after the sport utility vehicle (SUV) they were travelling in skidded at Kilometre 59 of Jalan Jerangau-Jabor, near Jerangau Felda here, yesterday. In the 6.10 pm incident, two of them died at the scene, while the other died at the Jerangau Health Clinic. Dungun police chief Supt Maizura Abdul Kadir, who confirmed the incident, said further details will follow.

items.title: Abuse Budi95, lose it: Finance Ministry to freeze cards over subsidised fuel misuse
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 24 — The Ministry of Finance (MOF) will block the cards of individuals found to have abused the Budi Madani RON95 (Budi95) assistance programme by filling subsidised RON95 petrol into empty barrels or containers, as has gone viral recently. MOF stressed that such action clearly violates regulations because it constitutes misuse of fuel subsidies under the Budi95 initiative and could affect the government’s efforts to ensure transparent, targeted and effective subsidy management. The government, the MOF said, will not compromise on any form of subsidy leakages and misuse, especially amid current uncertainties that require close control over fuel supply and prices.

items.title: JPJ suspends two officers over viral offences: one a double-line breach, another a fatal crash
items.summary: IPOH, March 24 — The Road Transport Department (JPJ) has suspended two of its personnel to facilitate investigations into two separate incidents involving the department’s vehicle, namely a viral video showing a vehicle crossing double lines, and a fatal crash case. He said the two incidents involved different situations, one related to a viral video showing a JPJ vehicle crossing double lines and the other involving a crash. “The first incident involves a viral video of a JPJ vehicle crossing double lines along the East-West Highway (JRTB), which falls under the department’s internal investigation.

items.title: A slap on the wrist: Existing legal gaps weaken crackdown on syndicated cable theft, among key challenges, says Prasarana
items.summary: “Law enforcement like the Royal Malaysia Police has been very helpful in curbing cable theft but their hands have been tied for a long time. Idzqandar said lenient legal deterrence has resulted in rising cases of cable thefts and repeated offenders. — Picture by Yusof Mat Isa Idzqandar said law enforcement authorities typically investigate cable theft incidents under Section 379 of the Penal Code for theft, but suspects are often ultimately prosecuted under Sections 447 for criminal trespass instead.

items.title: MOH confirms civil servants caught smoking at Rawang eatery, compounds imposed
items.summary: The MOH said compounds have been imposed on the individuals under Section 16(2) (No 4: smoking at eating places) of the Control of Smoking Products for Public Health Act 2024 (Act 852). Earlier today, a post went viral on X depicting individuals serving with the MOH smoking at a restaurant in Rawang. “The MOH greatly appreciates the public’s concerns and encourages the channelling of information or complaints through official MOH channels to strengthen enforcement to protect public health,” it added.

items.title: Sultan of Pahang urges public to stay vigilant amid hot, dry weather
items.summary: KUANTAN, March 23 — The Sultan of Pahang Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri’ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah has urged the public to take extra precautions amid the current hot and dry weather affecting the state. Al-Sultan Abdullah also urged the public to refrain from open burning, including the disposal of rubbish, dry leaves and garden waste, as it could trigger uncontrolled fires and worsen air quality. “Open burning is not only dangerous but can also cause haze and affect public health.

items.title: KL-Butterworth ETS delayed by over 2.5 hours after technical glitch at Rasa station
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 23 — Electric Train Service (ETS) EP9130 from KL Sentral to Butterworth is expected to experience a delay of about 157 minutes following a technical issue at Rasa station, Hulu Selangor. Keretapi Tanah Melayu Berhad (KTMB) said in a statement that due to the technical glitch, the train, which departed KL Sentral at 1.40 pm, is now expected to reach Butterworth Station at 8.22 pm instead of the original 5.45 pm. “The technical glitch at Rasa station occurred at 3.02 pm today,” said KTMB.
